architecture.summary: GM-PRM is a generative multimodal process reward model that extends PRMs from passive binary verifiers to active reasoning collaborators. It takes multimodal mathematical problems (image + text) and step-by-step reasoning chains, and generates interpretable multi-aspect critiques (step intent, image alignment, reasoning logic) plus a corrected version of the first erroneous step. The corrected step is fed back into a policy MLLM via the Refined-BoN test-time framework, which iteratively refines flawed reasoning trajectories and resamples to improve solution diversity and correctness.
trained_components: GM-PRM (Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct base, all parameters except frozen ViT encoder)

biblio.title: GM-PRM: A Generative Multimodal Process Reward Model for Multimodal Mathematical Reasoning

claims.statement: GM-PRM achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple multimodal math benchmarks, significantly boosting policy model performance with notable accuracy gains
GM-PRM requires only a 20K-sample training dataset for effective training
On WeMath, MiniCPM-V2.6-8B improves by +12.4 points with GM-PRM and Refined-BoN
Under Best-of-8 setting, GM-PRM delivers a gain of 4.9 points over Self-Consistency on MiniCPM-V2.6-8B on WeMath
Refined-BoN improves Pass@8 scores compared to standard BoN, increasing average Pass@8 values of MiniCPM-V2.6-8B by +0.9 points across five datasets
GM-PRM generalizes effectively to other types of multimodal mathematical problems even though it is primarily trained on plane geometry and function problems

Models with weaker baseline performance benefit more from the refinement mechanism of GM-PRM with Refined-BoN; MiniCPM-V2.6-8B demonstrates the most significant relative improvement (+21.0%), despite its lower initial score
MathVision improvements are more modest (+1.9–3.9 points), indicating that the visual reasoning component may already be relatively strong in baseline models
